sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Joe, presented with a swollen and painful right knee after a skiing accident on Saturday, where he performed a cartwheel and felt a pop in his knee.
He has been experiencing pain and swelling, and has been taking Advil and icing the area.
He denies any prior knee injuries.
Past medical history: The patient has no prior knee injuries.
Review of systems: The patient reports a bruised shoulder, which he attributes to landing on it during the fall.
Current medications: Advil.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's temperature is 97.9 degrees, heart rate is 64, respirations are 16, blood pressure is 110/74, and oxygen saturation is 98%.
Physical exam: The patient has edema and ecchymosis around the right knee, tenderness to palpation, and pain with flexion and extension.
He has full range of motion, but it feels tight and painful.
The patient's right shoulder has bruising and is tender to touch, but he can raise it without significant difficulty.
Test Results: X-ray of the right knee shows no fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a knee contusion and a possible shoulder injury.
Problem list: Knee contusion; Shoulder injury; [unknown].
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen 400mg every 6-8 hours as needed for pain management.
I advised the patient to rest the knee and apply ice for 20 minutes, 2-3 times a day.
I will order an X-ray of the right shoulder to rule out any potential injuries.
I will also have the patient follow up with me in a few days to re-evaluate the condition of the knee and shoulder.
Follow-up: The patient will be scheduled for a follow-up appointment in a few days to re-evaluate the condition of the knee and shoulder.
